title: Participant
content:
Participant
before. I think

You
Okay.

Participant
the retaliation is the much stronger of the 2 cases. Okay, and the reason I think it's stronger is because while, you know, our, our position is, is that, that full time, remote work, is the only effective, method, they're going to argue and say that partial, remote work, well, not, may not be your preferred accommodation, it was an accommodation that was effective, and you know, we could argue that and get into that, I think that's going to create confusion as opposed to simply. The retaliation side of it is, hey I requested an accommodation. You know, and then, all of a sudden, they didn't renew my contract, and I was let go,

You
Mm-hmm.

Participant
and, and all we have to prove for retaliation is that you, you exercise your protected right, which you did multiple times, by, by filing charges of discrimination, and requesting combination requests be re-reviewed, and the 2nd element is some adverse action, and they didn't renew your contract, and the 3rd element is a causal connection, so you have the timing of it. So in other words, the easiest way to, quote, skin the cat. I think is retaliation and it may be better because under the discrimination side of it. We have to prove that you, you had a disability, that you were qualified, meaning you could do the job with, with the accommodation. And so it creates this whole, like, all of a sudden now we're having doctors testify as to your medical condition and all this stuff, whereas the retaliation side, it's just simply. Hey, I requested an accommodation, and they fired me because of it.
